},\n descriptionLabel: {\n 'en-GB': 'Description:',\n 'el-GR': 'Περιγραφή:'\n },\n externalDetailsLabel: {\n 'en-GB': 'External Details:',\n 'el-GR': 'Εξωτερικές Λεπτομέρειες:'\n },\n orderIdColon: {\n 'en-GB': 'Order ID:',\n 'el-GR': 'Αριθμός Παραγγελίας:'\n },\n sourceColon: {\n 'en-GB': 'Source:',\n 'el-GR': 'Πηγή:'\n },\n returnUrlColon: {\n 'en-GB': 'Return URL:',\n 'el-GR': 'URL Επιστροφής:'\n },\n paymentRejectedMessage: {\n 'en-GB': 'Payment was rejected. You will be redirected back to the merchant.',\n 'el-GR': 'Η πληρωμή απορρίφθηκε. Θα ανακατευθυνθείτε πίσω στον έμπορο.'\n },\n redirectingCountdown: {\n 'en-GB': 'Redirecting to Juice Shop in',\n 'el-GR': 'Ανακατεύθυνση στο Juice Shop σε'\n },\n secondsText: {\n 'en-GB': 'seconds...',\n 'el-GR': 'δευτερόλεπτα...'\n },\n \n // Notification translations\n notificationExternalPaymentRequest: {\n 'en-GB': 'External Payment Request',\n 'el-GR': 'Αίτημα Εξωτερικής Πληρωμής'\n },\n paymentRequestFromSource: {\n 'en-GB': 'Payment request from {{source}} for ¤{{amount}}',\n 'el-GR': 'Αίτημα πληρωμής από {{source}} για ¤{{amount}}'\n },\n moneyRequestApproved: {\n 'en-GB': 'Money Request Approved',\n 'el-GR': 'Αίτημα Εγκρίθηκε'\n },\n paymentSent: {\n 'en-GB': 'Payment Sent',\n 'el-GR': 'Πληρωμή Στάλθηκε'\n },\n approvedYourRequestMessage: {\n 'en-GB': '{{approver}} approved your request for ¤{{amount}}{{description}}',\n 'el-GR': '{{approver}} ενέκρινε το αίτημά σας για ¤{{amount}}{{description}}'\n },\n youReceivedMessage: {\n 'en-GB': 'You received ¤{{amount}} from {{sender}}{{description}}',\n 'el-GR': 'Λάβατε ¤{{amount}} από {{sender}}{{description}}'\n },\n youSentMessage: {\n 'en-GB': 'You sent ¤{{amount}} to {{recipient}}{{description}}',\n 'el-GR': 'Στείλατε ¤{{amount}} σε {{recipient}}{{description}}'\n },\n paymentApprovedTitle: {\n 'en-GB': 'Payment Approved!',\n 'el-GR': 'Πληρωμή Εγκρίθηκε!'\n }\n};\n\n/**\n * I18n Context Type - React context interface for language management\n * \n * Defines the shape of the internationalization context providing:\n * - Current language state\n * - Language switching functionality \n * - Translation function for retrieving localized strings\n */\ninterface I18nContextType {\n language: Language;\n setLanguage: (lang: Language) => void;\n t: (key: keyof Translations) => string;\n}\n\n/**\n * I18n React Context - Global language state container\n * \n * Creates the React context for sharing internationalization state\n * across the entire component tree without prop drilling.\n */\nconst I18nContext = createContext<I18nContextType | null>(null);\n\n/**\n * useI18n Hook - Access internationalization functionality\n * \n * Custom React hook providing access to language state and translation\n * functions. Must be used within an I18nProvider component tree.\n * \n * @returns Object containing current language, setLanguage function, and t() translator\n * @throws Error if used outside of I18nProvider\n * \n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * function MyComponent() {\n * const { language, setLanguage, t } = useI18n();\n * \n * return (\n * <div>\n * <h1>{t('dashboard')}</h1>\n * <button onClick={() => setLanguage('el-GR')}>\n * Switch to Greek\n * </button>\n * </div>\n * );\n * }\n * ```\n */\nexport const useI18n = () => {\n const context = useContext(I18nContext);\n if (!context) {\n throw new Error('useI18n must be used within an I18nProvider');\n }\n return context;\n};\n\n/**\n * I18nProvider Component - Internationalization context provider\n * \n * Root provider component that manages global language state and provides\n * translation functionality to all child components. Handles:\n * - Language state management with localStorage persistence\n * - Translation function with fallback support\n * - Context value distribution throughout component tree\n * \n * Features:\n * - Automatic localStorage synchronization for user preferences\n * - Fallback to UK English for missing translations\n * - Type-safe translation key validation\n * \n * @param children - React children to wrap with i18n context\n * \n * @example\n * ```typescript\n * function App() {\n * return (\n * <I18nProvider>\n * <Router>\n * <Routes />\n * </Router>\n * </I18nProvider>\n * );\n * }\n * ```\n */\nexport function I18nProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {\n // Initialize language from localStorage with fallback to UK English\n const [language, setLanguage] = useState<Language>(() => {\n const saved = localStorage.getItem('whoopspay-language');\n return (saved === 'en-GB' || saved === 'el-GR') ? (saved as Language) : 'en-GB';\n });\n\n // Persist language changes to localStorage\n useEffect(() => {\n localStorage.setItem('whoopspay-language', language);\n }, [language]);\n\n /**\n * Translation Function - Retrieve localized strings\n * \n * Looks up translation keys in the current language with automatic\n * fallback to UK English for missing translations.\n * \n * @param key - Translation key from the Translations interface\n * @returns Localized string in current language or UK English fallback\n */\n const t = (key: keyof Translations): string => {\n return translations[key][language] || translations[key]['en-GB'];\n };\n\n const value = { language, setLanguage, t };\n\n return createElement(I18nContext.Provider, { value }, children);\n}\n\n/**\n * useLanguage Hook - Access i18n context\n * \n * Custom hook for accessing the internationalization context.\n * Provides language state and translation functionality to components.\n * \n * @returns I18nContext containing language, setLanguage, and t function\n * @throws Error if used outside of I18nProvider\n */\nexport function useLanguage() {\n const context = useContext(I18nContext);\n if (!context) {\n throw new Error('useLanguage must be used within an I18nProvider');\n }\n return context;\n}"],"mappings":"AAcA,SAAS,UAAU,WAAW,eAAe,YAAuB,qBAAqB;AAwjBzF,MAAM,eAA6B;AAAA;AAAA,EAEjC,WAAW;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,cAAc;AAAA,IACZ,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,QAAQ;AAAA,IACN,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,SAAS;AAAA,IACP,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,UAAU;AAAA,IACR,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,OAAO;AAAA,IACL,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA;AAAA,EAGA,OAAO;AAAA,IACL,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,QAAQ;AAAA,IACN,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,QAAQ;AAAA,IACN,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,QAAQ;AAAA,IACN,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,MAAM;AAAA,IACJ,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,MAAM;AAAA,IACJ,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,QAAQ;AAAA,IACN,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,SAAS;AAAA,IACP,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,QAAQ;AAAA,IACN,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,QAAQ;AAAA,IACN,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA;AAAA,EAGA,WAAW;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,cAAc;AAAA,IACZ,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,QAAQ;AAAA,IACN,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,WAAW;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,aAAa;AAAA,IACX,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,eAAe;AAAA,IACb,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,SAAS;AAAA,IACP,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA;AAAA,EAGA,SAAS;AAAA,IACP,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,SAAS;AAAA,IACP,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,kBAAkB;AAAA,IAChB,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,eAAe;AAAA,IACb,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA;AAAA,EAGA,eAAe;AAAA,IACb,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,cAAc;AAAA,IACZ,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,eAAe;AAAA,IACb,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,WAAW;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,iBAAiB;AAAA,IACf,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,iBAAiB;AAAA,IACf,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,aAAa;AAAA,IACX,SAAS;AAAA,IACT,SAAS;AAAA,EACX;AAAA,EACA,UAAU Other Info {"version":3,"sources":["queryClient.ts"],"sourcesContent":["/**\n * Query Client Configuration - Centralized API communication layer\n * \n * Provides standardized HTTP request handling, error management, and\n * caching strategies for the WhoopsPay application. Includes session\n * management and authentication state handling.\n */\nimport { QueryClient, QueryFunction } from \"@tanstack/react-query\";\n\n/**\n * HTTP Response Error Handler - Converts failed responses to errors\n * \n * Extracts meaningful error messages from HTTP responses and throws\n * standardized errors for consistent error handling throughout the app.\n * \n * @param res - HTTP Response object to validate\n * @throws Error with status code and response text\n */\nasync function throwIfResNotOk(res: Response) {\n if (!res.ok) {\n const text = (await res.text()) || res.statusText;\n throw new Error(`${res.status}: ${text}`);\n }\n}\n\n/**\n * API Request Helper - Standardized HTTP request function\n * \n * Handles all API communication with consistent headers, credentials,\n * and error handling. Automatically includes session cookies for\n * authentication and sets appropriate content types.\n * \n * @param url - API endpoint URL\n * @param method - HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)\n * @param data - Optional request body data (will be JSON stringified)\n * @returns Promise resolving to parsed JSON data\n * @throws Error if request fails or returns error status\n */\nexport async function apiRequest(\n url: string,\n method: string,\n data?: unknown | undefined,\n): Promise<any> {\n const bodyString = data ? JSON.stringify(data) : undefined;\n \n const res = await fetch(url, {\n method,\n headers: data ? { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" } : {},\n body: bodyString,\n credentials: \"include\", // Include session cookies for authentication\n });\n\n await throwIfResNotOk(res);\n \n // Handle empty responses for DELETE operations\n const contentType = res.headers.get(\"content-type\");\n if (contentType && contentType.includes(\"application/json\")) {\n return await res.json();\n }\n \n return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Unauthorized behavior configuration for query functions\n * Controls how authentication failures are handled in API requests\n */\ntype UnauthorizedBehavior = \"returnNull\" | \"throw\";\n\n/**\n * Query Function Factory - Creates configured query functions for TanStack Query\n * \n * Generates query functions with customizable unauthorized error handling.\n * Used to standardize API data fetching across the application with\n * consistent authentication and error management.\n * \n * @param options - Configuration object with unauthorized behavior setting\n * @returns QueryFunction configured for TanStack Query usage\n */\nexport const getQueryFn: <T>(options: {\n on401: UnauthorizedBehavior;\n}) => QueryFunction<T> =\n ({ on401: unauthorizedBehavior }) =>\n async ({ queryKey }) => {\n const res = await fetch(queryKey[0] as string, {\n credentials: \"include\", // Include session cookies for authentication\n });\n\n // Handle unauthorized responses based on configuration\n if (unauthorizedBehavior === \"returnNull\" && res.status === 401) {\n return null;\n }\n\n await throwIfResNotOk(res);\n return await res.json();\n };\n\n/**\n * Global Query Client - Centralized TanStack Query configuration\n * \n * Configures default behavior for all queries and mutations in the application:\n * - Automatic error handling for failed requests\n * - Session-based authentication with credential inclusion\n * - Disabled automatic refetching for educational stability\n * - Infinite stale time to prevent unnecessary re-requests\n * - Disabled retries to show immediate error states for learning\n * \n * Educational Note: In production, enable refetching and retries for better UX\n */\nexport const queryClient = new QueryClient({\n defaultOptions: {\n queries: {\n queryFn: getQueryFn({ on401: \"throw\" }), // Throw on unauthorized for immediate error handling\n refetchInterval: false, // Disable automatic background refetching\n refetchOnWindowFocus: false, // Disable refetch on window focus for demo stability\n staleTime: Infinity, // Data never becomes stale for educational consistency\n retry: false, // No retries to show error states immediately\n },\n mutations: {\n retry: false, // No mutation retries for immediate error feedback\n },\n },\n});\n"],"mappings":"AAOA,SAAS,mBAAkC;AAW3C,eAAe,gBAAgB,KAAe;AAC5C,MAAI,CAAC,IAAI,IAAI;AACX,UAAM,OAAQ,MAAM,IAAI,KAAK,KAAM,IAAI;AACvC,UAAM,IAAI,MAAM,GAAG,IAAI,MAAM,KAAK,IAAI,EAAE;AAAA,EAC1C;AACF;AAeA,sBAAsB,WACpB,KACA,QACA,MACc;AACd,QAAM,aAAa,OAAO,KAAK,UAAU,IAAI,IAAI;AAEjD,QAAM,MAAM,MAAM,MAAM,KAAK;AAAA,IAC3B;AAAA,IACA,SAAS,OAAO,EAAE,gBAAgB,mBAAmB,IAAI,CAAC;AAAA,IAC1D,MAAM;AAAA,IACN,aAAa;AAAA;AAAA,EACf,CAAC;AAED,QAAM,gBAAgB,GAAG;AAGzB,QAAM,cAAc,IAAI,QAAQ,IAAI,cAAc;AAClD,MAAI,eAAe,YAAY,SAAS,kBAAkB,GAAG;AAC3D,WAAO,MAAM,IAAI,KAAK;AAAA,EACxB;AAEA,SAAO;AACT;AAkBO,aAAM,aAGX,CAAC,EAAE,OAAO,qBAAqB,MAC/B,OAAO,EAAE,SAAS,MAAM;AACtB,QAAM,MAAM,MAAM,MAAM,SAAS,CAAC,GAAa;AAAA,IAC7C,aAAa;AAAA;AAAA,EACf,CAAC;AAGD,MAAI,yBAAyB,gBAAgB,IAAI,WAAW,KAAK;AAC/D,WAAO;AAAA,EACT;AAEA,QAAM,gBAAgB,GAAG;AACzB,SAAO,MAAM,IAAI,KAAK;AACxB;AAcK,aAAM,cAAc,IAAI,YAAY;AAAA,EACzC,gBAAgB;AAAA,IACd,SAAS;AAAA,MACP,SAAS,WAAW,EAAE,OAAO,QAAQ,CAAC;AAAA;AAAA,MACtC,iBAAiB;AAAA;AAAA,MACjB,sBAAsB;AAAA;AAAA,MACtB,WAAW;AAAA;AAAA,MACX,OAAO;AAAA;AAAA,IACT;AAAA,IACA,WAAW;AAAA,MACT,OAAO;AAAA;AAAA,IACT;AAAA,EACF;AACF,CAAC;","names":[]}

Other Info {"version":3,"sources":["utils.ts"],"sourcesContent":["import { clsx, type ClassValue } from \"clsx\"\nimport { twMerge } from \"tailwind-merge\"\n\nexport function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {\n return twMerge(clsx(inputs))\n}\n"],"mappings":"AAAA,SAAS,YAA6B;AACtC,SAAS,eAAe;AAEjB,gBAAS,MAAM,QAAsB;AAC1C,SAAO,QAAQ,KAAK,MAAM,CAAC;AAC7B;","names":[]}

Instances 251
Solution
Manually confirm that the Base64 data does not leak sensitive information, and that the data cannot be aggregated/used to exploit other vulnerabilities.
Reference https://projects.webappsec.org/w/page/13246936/Information%20Leakage
CWE Id 319
WASC Id 13
Plugin Id 10094
Informational
 Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in URL
Description
The request appeared to contain sensitive information leaked in the URL. This can violate PCI and most organizational compliance policies. You can configure the list of strings for this check to add or remove values specific to your environment.
URL http://localhost:5000/?token=m2s2fgeCT-Ga
Method GET
Parameter token
Attack
Evidence token
Other Info The URL contains potentially sensitive information. The following string was found via the pattern: token token
Instances 1
Solution
Do not pass sensitive information in URIs.
Reference
CWE Id 598
WASC Id 13
Plugin Id 10024
Informational

Instances 103
Solution
Remove all comments that return information that may help an attacker and fix any underlying problems they refer to.
Reference
CWE Id 615
WASC Id 13
Plugin Id 10027
Informational
 Modern Web Application
Description
The application appears to be a modern web application. If you need to explore it automatically then the Ajax Spider may well be more effective than the standard one.
URL http://localhost:5000/
Method GET
Parameter
Attack
Evidence <script type="module"> import { createHotContext } from "/@vite/client"; const hot = createHotContext("/__dummy__runtime-error-plugin"); function sendError(error) { if (!(error instanceof Error)) { error = new Error("(unknown runtime error)"); } const serialized = { message: error.message, stack: error.stack, }; hot.send("runtime-error-plugin:error", serialized); } window.addEventListener("error", (evt) => { sendError(evt.error); }); window.addEventListener("unhandledrejection", (evt) => { sendError(evt.reason); }); </script>
Other Info No links have been found while there are scripts, which is an indication that this is a modern web application.

URL http://localhost:5000/api/login
Method POST
Parameter
Attack
Evidence 401
Other Info
Instances 72
Solution
The content may be marked as storable by ensuring that the following conditions are satisfied:

The request method must be understood by the cache and defined as being cacheable ("GET", "HEAD", and "POST" are currently defined as cacheable)

The response status code must be understood by the cache (one of the 1XX, 2XX, 3XX, 4XX, or 5XX response classes are generally understood)

The "no-store" cache directive must not appear in the request or response header fields

For caching by "shared" caches such as "proxy" caches, the "private" response directive must not appear in the response

For caching by "shared" caches such as "proxy" caches, the "Authorization" header field must not appear in the request, unless the response explicitly allows it (using one of the "must-revalidate", "public", or "s-maxage" Cache-Control response directives)

In addition to the conditions above, at least one of the following conditions must also be satisfied by the response:

It must contain an "Expires" header field

It must contain a "max-age" response directive

For "shared" caches such as "proxy" caches, it must contain a "s-maxage" response directive

It must contain a "Cache Control Extension" that allows it to be cached

It must have a status code that is defined as cacheable by default (200, 203, 204, 206, 300, 301, 404, 405, 410, 414, 501).
Reference https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7234
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7231
https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec13.html
CWE Id 524
WASC Id 13
Plugin Id 10049
Informational
 Sec-Fetch-Dest Header is Missing
Description
Specifies how and where the data would be used. For instance, if the value is audio, then the requested resource must be audio data and not any other type of resource.
URL http://localhost:5000/
Method GET
Parameter Sec-Fetch-Dest

URL http://localhost:5000/api/login
Method POST
Parameter Sec-Fetch-Dest
Attack
Evidence
Other Info
Instances 394
Solution
Ensure that Sec-Fetch-Dest header is included in request headers.
Reference https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Sec-Fetch-Dest
CWE Id 352
WASC Id 9
Plugin Id 90005
Informational
 Sec-Fetch-Mode Header is Missing
Description
Allows to differentiate between requests for navigating between HTML pages and requests for loading resources like images, audio etc.
URL http://localhost:5000/
Method GET
Parameter Sec-Fetch-Mode
Attack
Evidence
Other Info

URL http://localhost:5000/api/login
Method POST
Parameter Sec-Fetch-Mode
Attack
Evidence
Other Info
Instances 394
Solution
Ensure that Sec-Fetch-Mode header is included in request headers.
Reference https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Sec-Fetch-Mode
CWE Id 352
WASC Id 9
Plugin Id 90005
Informational
 Sec-Fetch-Site Header is Missing
Description
Specifies the relationship between request initiator's origin and target's origin.

Parameter Sec-Fetch-Site

URL http://localhost:5000/api/login
Method POST
Parameter Sec-Fetch-Site
Attack
Evidence
Other Info
Instances 394
Solution
Ensure that Sec-Fetch-Site header is included in request headers.
Reference https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Sec-Fetch-Site
CWE Id 352
WASC Id 9
Plugin Id 90005
Informational
 Sec-Fetch-User Header is Missing
Description
Specifies if a navigation request was initiated by a user.

URL http://localhost:5000/src/pages/wallet.tsx
Method GET
Parameter Sec-Fetch-User
Attack
Evidence
Other Info
URL http://localhost:5000/api/login
Method POST
Parameter Sec-Fetch-User
Attack
Evidence
Other Info
Instances 394
Solution
Ensure that Sec-Fetch-User header is included in user initiated requests.
Reference https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Sec-Fetch-User
CWE Id 352
WASC Id 9
Plugin Id 90005
Informational
 Storable and Cacheable Content
Description
The response contents are storable by caching components such as proxy servers, and may be retrieved directly from the cache, rather than from the origin server by the caching servers, in response to similar requests from other users. If the response data is sensitive, personal or user-specific, this may result in sensitive information being leaked. In some cases, this may even result in a user gaining complete control of the session of another user, depending on the configuration of the caching components in use in their environment. This is primarily an issue where "shared" caching servers such as "proxy" caches are configured on the local network. This configuration is typically found in corporate or educational environments, for instance.
URL http://localhost:5000/
Method GET
Parameter
Attack
Evidence
Other Info In the absence of an explicitly specified caching lifetime directive in the response, a liberal lifetime heuristic of 1 year was assumed. This is permitted by rfc7234.

URL http://localhost:5000/src/main.tsx?v=ZxHSNF1nVBvboCptZxIBR

Other Info
Instances 323
Solution
Validate that the response does not contain sensitive, personal or user-specific information. If it does, consider the use of the following HTTP response headers, to limit, or prevent the content being stored and retrieved from the cache by another user:

Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate, private

Pragma: no-cache

Expires: 0

This configuration directs both HTTP 1.0 and HTTP 1.1 compliant caching servers to not store the response, and to not retrieve the response (without validation) from the cache, in response to a similar request.
Reference https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7234
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7231
https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec13.html
CWE Id 524
WASC Id 13
Plugin Id 10049
Informational
 Storable but Non-Cacheable Content
Description
The response contents are storable by caching components such as proxy servers, but will not be retrieved directly from the cache, without validating the request upstream, in response to similar requests from other users.
